hint: When multiplying a negative by a positive, the result is negative.
explanation:
The signs are different (negative and positive), so the result is negative.
Multiply the absolute values:
$$4 \times 3 = 12$$
Apply the sign:
$$-4 \times 3 = -12$$

hint: When dividing a negative by a positive, the result is negative.
explanation:
The signs are different, so the result is negative.
Divide the absolute values:
$$15 \div 3 = 5$$
Apply the sign:
$$-15 \div 3 = -5$$

hint: When multiplying a positive by a negative, the result is negative.
explanation:
The signs are different, so the result is negative.
Multiply the absolute values:
$$6 \times 5 = 30$$
Apply the sign:
$$6 \times (-5) = -30$$

hint: When dividing a positive by a negative, the result is negative.
explanation:
The signs are different, so the result is negative.
Divide the absolute values:
$$20 \div 4 = 5$$
Apply the sign:
$$20 \div (-4) = -5$$

hint: When multiplying two negatives, the result is positive.
explanation:
Both signs are negative, so the result is positive.
Multiply the absolute values:
$$6 \times 5 = 30$$
Apply the sign:
$$-6 \times (-5) = 30$$

hint: When dividing two negatives, the result is positive.
explanation:
Both signs are negative, so the result is positive.
Divide the absolute values:
$$18 \div 3 = 6$$
Apply the sign:
$$-18 \div (-3) = 6$$

hint: When multiplying two negatives, the result is positive.
explanation:
Both signs are negative, so the result is positive.
Multiply the absolute values:
$$8 \times 7 = 56$$
Apply the sign:
$$-8 \times (-7) = 56$$

hint: When dividing two negatives, the result is positive.
explanation:
Both signs are negative, so the result is positive.
Divide the absolute values:
$$24 \div 6 = 4$$
Apply the sign:
$$-24 \div (-6) = 4$$

hint: Work from left to right. First multiply, then divide.
explanation:
Work from left to right:
$$(-3) \times 4 = -12$$
Then divide:
$$-12 \div (-6) = 2$$
(Two negatives give a positive result.)

hint: Work from left to right, applying sign rules at each step.
explanation:
Work from left to right:
$$(-2) \times (-3) = 6$$
(Two negatives give a positive.)
Then:
$$6 \times (-4) = -24$$
(Positive times negative gives a negative.)

hint: Apply order of operations: perform both multiplications before adding.
explanation:
Perform the multiplications first:
$$3 \times (-4) = -12$$
$$(-2) \times 5 = -10$$
Then add:
$$-12 + (-10) = -12 - 10 = -22$$

hint: Work from left to right. Divide first, then multiply.
explanation:
Work from left to right:
$$(-15) \div 3 = -5$$
Then multiply:
$$-5 \times (-2) = 10$$
(Two negatives give a positive result.)
